session_summary: No-event-time architectural pivot. v0 simplifies dramatically: Beava is Redis with stateful operators, processing-time only — no event-time anywhere, no watermarks, no joins (any kind), no PIT. Five answered design questions locked the shape. Five phase dirs archived (14 streaming-correctness, 14.1 streaming-modifiability, 15 event-time-PIT, 15-snapshot-io-off-main-thread, 14-per-stream-locks-dashmap-concurrency). New permanent memory `project_redis_shaped_no_event_time_ever` written; `project_stateful_architecture` Decision 2 (PIT) marked SUPERSEDED. ROADMAP retitled Phase 12, archived 12.5/14/14.1/15, reworked 17, inserted NEW Phase 12.6 (v0 surface reduction — biggest v0 blocker) and NEW Phase 25 (session windows — v0.1+). PROJECT/REQUIREMENTS/STATE/CORRECTNESS-PATH all swept to match. Single doc-only commit `2d58d80`. No code changes.
next_focus: v0 polish via Phase 12.6 surface reduction (architectural cleanup) → Phase 13 minimum subset (packaging + quickstart + readme + website final) → ship

Identified Phase 14 dir collision: `14-per-stream-locks-dashmap-concurrency` (pre-pivot stale work) vs `14-streaming-correctness` (canonical roadmap entry); archived stale dir + verified resolver

Reviewed all 4 streaming-correctness plans + surfaced D-08 drift (Phase 13.3 RefCell/borrow_mut narrative dead — apply path is now ServerV18 mio); identified 8 drift items (3 critical/medium, 5 minor)

User cascade pivot decisions: defer Phase 14 → defer all joins → remove all watermarks (permanent) → remove event-time entirely (permanent) → session windows are simple incremental → mio is the only hot-path entry

Answered 5 design questions: (Q1) event_time_ms REMOVED from wire entirely [user override of recommend-keep]; (Q2) windowed ops switch to now_ms() server clock — Path X preserves 55-op catalogue; (Q3) session windows close on BOTH lazy-on-query AND flip-on-next-event-after-gap; (Q4) latest closed session retained per (entity, feature); (Q5) bv.union deferred with joins to v0.1+
Memory updates: NEW project_redis_shaped_no_event_time_ever.md (permanent commitment); MODIFIED project_stateful_architecture.md (Decision 2 PIT marked SUPERSEDED); UPDATED MEMORY.md index

Archived 5 phase dirs via git mv to `.planning/phases/_archived-*`: 14-streaming-correctness, 14.1-streaming-modifiability, 15-event-time-pit, 15-snapshot-io-off-main-thread, 14-per-stream-locks-dashmap-concurrency

key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.decision: Permanent: no event-time anywhere in Beava — ever
key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.implication: All future plans/discussions must respect this. Reviving requires explicit user override + new ADR.

key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.decision: Permanent: no watermarks, no late-event correction, no PIT temporal store, no joins (event↔event/event↔table/table↔table)
mio data plane is the only hot-path entry; legacy axum scheduled for removal in Phase 12.6
key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.implication: All push/get/upsert/delete dispatches via apply_shard.rs::dispatch_*_sync; tokio sidecar only for admin endpoints

key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.decision: Session windows replace event-time grouping for activity aggregation
key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.implication: New Phase 25 in ROADMAP (v0.1+); 5-7 plans; not v0 ship-blocker

open_product_questions_to_resolve_before.what: Users should be able to use the Beava SDK as if it were polars. The SDK chain (`bv.col("x")` / `.filter` / `.select` / `.with_columns` / `.cast` / `.fillna` / `.group_by().agg()`) is already polars-shaped — the question is closing the polars-parity gap so a polars-native data scientist is instantly productive without learning a foreign API. NOT pandas style — Beava's expression-first immutable shape is intentionally polars-shaped, not pandas-shaped.
open_product_questions_to_resolve_before.why_now: v0 audience is Priya YC fintech CTO + her data-scientist team. Polars is the modern dataframe choice for that audience (Rust-backed, expression-first, immutable). Beava is structurally polars-shaped already; the question is whether the parity is tight enough that a polars user reads the SDK and says 'oh, this is just polars-for-streaming' without friction. Existing memory `project_pandas_mapping_revisit` flagged that beava.dev homepage uses simplified `events.agg(key=, window=, ...)` form — that pandas-leaning framing should pivot to polars positioning since the actual API is polars-shaped.
open_aspects: Polars-parity gap analysis — every polars idiom (`pl.col`, `.select`, `.filter`, `.with_columns`, `.group_by().agg()`, `.over()`, `.cast()`, `.fillna()`, expression chaining, lazy vs eager) checked against Beava's actual SDK; flag where Beava deviates and decide keep-vs-fix

Aggregation chain shape — `events.group_by(...).agg(...)` (current; polars-matching) is correct; consider whether Beava's register-then-push model has any awkward edges vs polars `.lazy().group_by().agg().collect()`

Lazy-vs-eager / register-then-push framing — polars HAS a lazy mode (`pl.LazyFrame`) so the deferred-eval mental model is closer to polars than to pandas. Frame Beava's `app.register(...)` as 'collect()-equivalent that ships the plan to the server'.

Polars `.over(partitions)` window expressions — Beava's per-entity aggregation is conceptually `events.group_by(entity).agg(...).over(window)`. Does the SDK surface this clearly enough that a polars user maps it instantly?
Output interop — does `app.get(...)` return something that can be `pl.DataFrame.from_dicts(...)`-friendly? `FeatureResult` shape vs polars-friendliness.
Recipe parity — every common polars recipe (group_by + agg, rolling windows via `.over()`, expression chains) should have a Beava equivalent in docs
beava.dev positioning sweep — homepage + guide pages should pitch as 'polars for streaming features' not 'pandas for streaming features' (existing pandas-mapping is misframed)
open_product_questions_to_resolve_before.phase_target: Phase 13 docs/SDK polish (positioning + recipes); minor API touchpoints in Phase 12.6 if gaps surface during the SDK sweep

open_product_questions_to_resolve_before.topic: Schema evolution UX (additive + warn/block on destructive)
open_product_questions_to_resolve_before.what: Users need clear UX for: (a) adding new fields — works seamlessly via additive registration; (b) REMOVING fields — server should warn AND block on destructive changes with a clear, human-readable explanation (currently 409 with structured JSON); (c) adding new pipelines/derivations — works additively. The 'warn and block' part is the polish gap.
open_product_questions_to_resolve_before.why_now: v0 launch will hit this on day one. Users iterating on features will try to remove a field; if the error is just `409 {error: {code: 'destructive_change', diff: {...}}}`, that's friction. Want: clear human-readable message + optional preview mode + obvious recovery path (e.g. 'rename the descriptor + re-register' or 'use force=True after explicit confirmation' — TBD).
open_aspects: Error message format — 409 body shape: structured JSON only, or also a `human_message` field?
Preview mode — `app.validate(*descriptors, against_server=True)` that returns the diff WITHOUT registering? Or HTTP `POST /register/dry-run`?
Force-override — does v0 allow `force=True` to bypass destructive-change block (with explicit acknowledgment) or is the only path 'rename + re-register'?
Adding new pipeline UX — should be friction-free; currently is per additive registration. Validate this end-to-end with a real dataframe-style add-derivation flow.
Warning vs error distinction — is there a class of changes that should warn-not-block (e.g. type widening int→f64)? Out-of-scope for v0?
open_product_questions_to_resolve_before.phase_target: Phase 12.6 (server-side error message work since wire schema is bumping anyway) + Phase 13 SDK polish (preview mode + docs)

open_product_questions_to_resolve_before.topic: Recovery benchmark — finalize an easy-to-run reproducible bench
open_product_questions_to_resolve_before.what: Users (and CI) should be able to easily run a recovery benchmark to validate the ≤ 30s RTO at 10GB state on NVMe target. Currently the target is documented but there's no reproducible harness. Need: a `beava-bench-recovery` (or similar) binary or script that loads N entities, snapshots, replays WAL, and reports RTO + memory + replay rate.
open_product_questions_to_resolve_before.why_now: v0 launch will get questioned on durability claims. 'Show me' is the right answer — provide a one-command bench. Also: the same harness validates that recovery works correctly post-Phase-12.6 (after wire schema bump + WAL record schema bump, recovery must handle both old + new schemas for one release cycle, or hard cutover).
open_aspects: Harness shape — new `beava-bench-recovery` binary, or a flag on `beava-bench-v18`?
Workload knobs — entity count, feature count per entity, WAL size, on-disk vs tmpfs, fsync mode (Periodic vs PerEvent)
Output format — same row format as `.planning/throughput-baselines.md` for consistency, or separate `recovery-baselines.md`?
CI integration — does this run in CI on every PR, or only on phase-13 ship-gate runs?
Schema-version handoff testing — recovery harness should also test old-schema-WAL → new-schema-replay path during the Phase 12.6 wire bump
open_product_questions_to_resolve_before.phase_target: Phase 13 perf-gate work (alongside throughput rebaseline + Hetzner sweep)

next_action_recommended: /gsd-discuss-phase 12.6 — gather scope-specific decisions for v0 surface reduction. Discuss should surface gray areas: (a) TestServerV18 harness shape — drop-in replacement vs new test pattern; (b) wire schema bump strategy — new version handle old+new for one release vs hard cutover; (c) dead-code threshold for 'sweep complete' (cargo-deadcode % target); (d) smoke-test fate per file (migrate vs delete vs replicate elsewhere); (e) REQUIREMENTS sweep depth (touch every event_time/join reference vs just the SDK-JOIN section); (f) docs touch scope (beava.dev guide pages, recipes — some require website work alongside); (g) phase-12-joins branch handling during merge — joins plumbing is now dead architecture; (h) plan ordering — kill axum first vs strip event-time first vs interleave; (i) does Phase 12.6 also touch the destructive-schema-change error UX (Q2 in open_product_questions) or does that defer to Phase 13?
